manifest.name: Code Intent Discovery
manifest.description: This manifest utilizes a Code Intent analyzer to generate discovery-optimized metadata for 322 files across Go, Shell, and YAML. It tracks action-object keywords, functional parent domains, and interface mechanisms to enable intelligent agent navigation and understanding of the codebase.

analyzers.name: Code Intent
analyzers.description: Generates a discovery-optimized summary and metadata, including Action-Object keywords, functional parent domains, interface types, and logic constraints.

fields.name: purpose
fields.description: A change-centric summary starting with "Modify this file to change..." or "Use this file to change...".

fields.name: type
fields.description: The standardized category of the file (e.g., `source_code`, `documentation`, `blog_post`). Must be a value defined in `keyword-map.json` or a standard default.

fields.name: dates
fields.description: An array of date strings formatted as `yyyy-mm-dd_type` (e.g., `["2023-10-27_published", "2023-11-05_modified"]`). Null if no dates.

fields.name: keywords
fields.description: An array of 3-7 specific, high-discrimination keywords. MUST be "Action-Object" pairs. MUST exclude stop words. Quality over quantity.

fields.name: parent_keywords
fields.description: An array of 2-5 broader, functional domain keywords that group the specific keywords.

fields.name: key_entities
fields.description: A list of the most important named components within the file.

fields.name: mechanism
fields.description: The interface/pattern type (e.g., `cli-command`, `rest-api`, `webhook-handler`, `factory-pattern`). Null if not applicable.

fields.name: logic_constraints
fields.description: A list of hardcoded business logic or configuration constants found in the file. Null if none found.

Implements a buffered file logger that writes timestamped, level-tagged entries to a turn-specific log file (turn-N/post-process.log) for post-processing audit trails. Use this file to change how post-processing steps are recorded or to adjust log formatting and flushing behavior.

Orchestrates the post-change validation and metadata enrichment phase, performing three-way validation (AI claims vs Git truth vs Metadata presence), injecting provenance headers, and finalizing session state. Use this file to change the validation logic, provenance recording behavior, or the final aggregation of turn results.
